content: Antiviral treatment recommended for all hospitalized patients, severe/progressive illness, and high-risk outpatients (age ≥65, <2 years, pregnancy, chronic conditions, immunocompromise, BMI ≥40). Initiate within 48 hours of symptom onset (benefit still possible if started later in severe cases). Options: oseltamivir 75mg BID x5 days (oral, preferred for most); zanamivir 10mg BID x5 days (inhaled, avoid in asthma/COPD); peramivir 600mg IV single dose; baloxavir single dose (cap-dependent endonuclease inhibitor, avoid with dairy/cations). Chemoprophylaxis: oseltamivir or zanamivir for post-exposure prophylaxis in high-risk individuals. Supportive care: antipyretics (avoid aspirin in children due to Reye syndrome), hydration. Antibiotics only for bacterial superinfection.
